events.title: US and Iran Trade Strikes in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The US military conducted strikes on missile sites and command centers in response to attacks on ships, while Iran reported retaliatory attacks on US bases. This exchange highlights the ongoing tensions and potential for wider conflict in the region.

events.title: Tanker Traffic Slows in Strait of Hormuz Amid US-Iran Clashes
events.desc: Daily tanker traffic in the Strait of Hormuz has reportedly slowed following recent exchanges of hostilities between the US and Iran. The incidents have renewed concerns about the recovery of global oil supplies and shipping routes.

events.title: UN Agency Rejects Iran's Claim of Sovereignty Over Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The UN shipping agency's governing council has agreed that countries should reject Iran's attempts to impose sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and its unilateral decision to establish a body to control traffic through the waterway. This follows recent clashes between the US and Iran.

events.title: Headteacher Among Four Killed in Israeli Attack on Lebanon
events.desc: A headteacher was among four individuals killed in the deadliest Israeli attack on Lebanon since the recent ceasefire. The victims were traveling in a car after visiting their family home.

events.title: Syrian Authorities Capture IS-Linked Cell Behind Damascus Bombings
events.desc: Syrian officials announced the capture of an Islamic State-linked cell responsible for two bomb blasts that occurred during French President Emmanuel Macron's visit to Damascus. The blasts wounded 18 people.

events.title: Explainer: Islamic State group linked to Damascus bombings
events.desc: Preliminary investigations suggest the cell behind recent bombings in Damascus, near a hotel visited by French President Macron, is affiliated with the Islamic State group. The bombings wounded 18 people.

events.title: Sudan's El Obeid Faces Intensifying RSF Drone Attacks
events.desc: The city of El Obeid in Sudan is experiencing escalating drone attacks attributed to the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), indicating a continuation and potential intensification of the conflict in the region.

events.title: Palestinians Mourn Gaza World Cup Screenings Organizer Killed in Israeli Strike
events.desc: Mohammed al-Wahidi, an aid worker and organizer of World Cup screenings in Gaza, was killed in an Israeli strike. He had become a prominent humanitarian figure during the recent conflict.

events.title: US-Iran Escalation Threatens Oil Supply Recovery, Warns IEA
events.desc: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has warned that the recent escalation between the US and Iran poses a threat to the recovery of global oil supplies. Sources suggest potential for reviving a ceasefire amid diplomatic efforts.

events.title: Iran Nuclear and Military Damage Revealed After Satellite Images Released
events.desc: Restricted satellite images have revealed damage to Iranian nuclear sites and military infrastructure, including ballistic missile sites and naval bases, following recent escalations.

events.title: US and Iran Trade Strikes After Tankers Hit in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: Following attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, the US military states it targeted Iranian missile sites and command centers, while Iran claims to have attacked US bases. This signifies a direct kinetic exchange between the two nations.

events.title: Trump Threatens More Strikes on Iran; Tehran Warns of Response
events.desc: US President Trump indicated that further strikes on Iran are probable, stating that any retaliatory action would be swift. Iran has issued warnings of a 'fearless' response, escalating tensions between the two countries.

events.title: Iran's Nuclear Program and the Fragile US-Iran Deal
events.desc: The framework agreement between the US and Iran, which included provisions on Iran's nuclear program, appears to be unraveling quickly. Mutual claims of violations and bad faith threaten the viability of the deal and could impact nuclear non-proliferation efforts.
